content:
What Anthropic’s “Golden Gate Claude” and Meta’s “Eiffel Tower Llama” teach us about the weird science of AI lobotomies.

6 min read
Just now

--

Imagine asking an AI to help you debug a Java program.
Instead of explaining why your NullPointerException happened, it suddenly replies:
“Before we discuss Java, have you considered the unmatched elegance of the Eiffel Tower? Its iron framework represents one of humanity’s greatest engineering achievements…”
You stop.
Maybe it misunderstood.
So you ask it for a chocolate cake recipe.
It responds:
“Chocolate cake is wonderful, but the Eiffel Tower stands taller than most cakes ever could. Let us appreciate its intricate lattice design…”
Press enter or click to view image in full size

AI generated imageThen you ask for relationship advice.
It says:
“A healthy relationship should be as enduring as the Eiffel Tower. Also, you should probably visit it.”
At this point you’d probably assume the model was broken.
Because it was.
